artPrompt: A brooding nighttime waterfront of looming cargo cranes and weathered docks slick with rain, black water lapping at barnacled pilings, a lone moored ship silhouetted against drifting harbor mist. Pools of amber lamplight spill across the wet planks where no figures stand, only long shadows and coiled mooring rope. Mono-black noir of ink shadow, smoky brown, and warm lamp glow, a hushed underworld harbor, composed wide in a 4:3 landscape frame, rendered as a vibrant gouache storybook illustration — saturated matte color, simplified flat-ish shapes, soft painterly edges, decorative.
name: Whateverville Waterfront
text:
Whateverville Waterfront enters the battlefield tapped unless you control another Goblin.
{T}: Add {B}.
{1}{B}, {T}, Sacrifice Whateverville Waterfront: Each opponent sacrifices a creature. The harbor keeps its secrets and its concrete shoes.
flavorText: The Don owns every dock, every crane, and every union that loads every ship. Especially the union.
